subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Build a Unity player (auto-routes between live editor via IPC and headless batchmode).
notes: `unictl build --wait` streams normalized lifecycle JSON; do not parse raw progress-file states such as done or aborted.
Trigger responses include job_id, status_command, cancel_command, poll_interval_ms, and terminal_states.
Successful low-confidence custom build results may still exit 0 but include suspicious=true and recommended_action.

subcommands.name: compile
subcommands.description: Headless batchmode compile + .meta generation.

subcommands.name: command
subcommands.description: Invoke a builtin tool exposed by the Unity Editor via IPC.
notes: For editor_log only, --format text emits data.lines, data.matches[].text, or errors groups as raw text for shell pipelines.
editor_log requires the current Unity session to have been started through unictl editor open/restart; stale or missing project logs return data.requires_editor_restart=true.

If editor-side workflows fail while editor_log action=errors reports C# errors, callers should branch on error.kind=editor_compile_error_state and fix compile errors before retrying compile/refresh/wait/test workflows.

Unsafe editor-side workflows require CLI and Unity UPM package version compatibility. Branch on unictl_cli_too_old to update the CLI, or unictl_upm_too_old/unictl_upm_version_unknown to run unictl init --version <cli_version> --force and restart the editor.

When a GameObject path is unknown in the live editor, use hierarchy_tree first: unictl command hierarchy_tree -p target=live -p include_components=true -p filter_component=EventSystem. It lists loaded scenes and Play Mode DontDestroyOnLoad roots.

subcommands.name: schema
subcommands.description: Emit machine-readable command contracts for agents and automation.

subcommands.name: describe-all
subcommands.description: Deprecated alias for unictl schema.

subcommands.name: doctor
subcommands.description: Environment diagnostics. Returns typed error.kind on failure.

subcommands.name: editor
subcommands.description: Unity editor lifecycle and live editor control (status | open | quit | restart | compile | play | stop | refresh).

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Show Unity editor process, IPC reachability, and rich ready-state fields.

subcommands.name: open
subcommands.description: Open the Unity editor; supports --wait [idle|playing|compiling|reloading|reachable] and --timeout.

subcommands.name: quit
subcommands.description: Quit the Unity editor.

subcommands.name: restart
subcommands.description: Restart the Unity editor.

subcommands.name: compile
subcommands.description: Trigger in-editor recompile; supports --wait [idle|playing|compiling|reloading|reachable] and --timeout.

subcommands.name: play
subcommands.description: Enter Play Mode; supports --wait [idle|playing|compiling|reloading|reachable] and --timeout.

subcommands.name: stop
subcommands.description: Exit Play Mode; supports --wait [idle|playing|compiling|reloading|reachable] and --timeout.

subcommands.name: refresh
subcommands.description: Run AssetDatabase.Refresh; supports --wait [idle|playing|compiling|reloading|reachable] and --timeout.

subcommands.name: health
subcommands.description: Lightweight IPC ping against the live editor.

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Add or update the com.unictl.editor manifest reference. Unity resolves/imports it on editor refresh, editor restart, or batch compile.

subcommands.name: input
subcommands.description: Unity Input System lifecycle commands.

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Switch the Unity Input System handler (legacy | new | both).

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: Deploy and signing-time settings.

subcommands.name: android
subcommands.description: Android-specific deploy commands.

subcommands.name: keystore
subcommands.description: Android keystore commands.

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Configure Android keystore path and alias.

subcommands.name: scripting
subcommands.description: Unity scripting backend settings.

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Toggle scripting backend (mono | il2cpp) per platform.

subcommands.name: settings
subcommands.description: Unity project settings escape hatches.

subcommands.name: raw-set
subcommands.description: Edit a Unity project setting by raw key; requires --no-warranty.

subcommands.name: wait
subcommands.description: Block until the editor reaches a target state.

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: Print unictl CLI version.

subcommands.name: capabilities
subcommands.description: Print offline capabilities JSON (this document).

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Run Unity tests. Uses editor lane (IPC) when the editor is reachable; otherwise auto-routes to batchmode. Batch EditMode uses the bundled -executeMethod/TestRunnerApi runner.

subcommands.name: wait
subcommands.description: Wait for an existing editor-lane test_run job; does not start tests.

notes: Unity native exit codes are NOT trusted directly; unictl remaps based on XML + stderr patterns.
Successful output includes lane=batch|editor. Editor lane also includes job_id, progress_file, and log_file pointing to the current editor session log.
results_file is the source of truth for test failures and thrown test exceptions; log_file is for Unity/editor diagnostics such as Debug.LogError, Debug.LogException, and unictl callbacks.
Default lane routing: --batch omitted + editor running → IPC via internal test_run tool; --batch omitted + editor not running → batchmode.
--batch is force-batch and fails if the target editor is already running.
If you already started raw command test_run, use unictl test wait <job-id>; do not grep the progress file text.
For PlayMode editor-lane diagnostics, inspect domain reload state with unictl editor status; domain_reload.domain_reload_enabled=false means Disable Domain Reload is active.
Batchmode lane: --batch required or editor not running → headless Unity process using Unictl.BatchTestRunner.RunFromCommandLine.

builtins.name: test_run
builtins.description: Internal editor-lane test tool. Prefer unictl test; raw callers must parse the progress file as JSON, not grep text.

results_path.description: Absolute path for NUnit XML output

job_id.description: Caller-assigned UUID for progress file naming

allow_reload_active.description: Force PlayMode + Full Reload (dangerous; may hang)

builtins.name: build_project
builtins.description: Build a Unity player; dual-lane auto-routing.

builtins.name: build_status
builtins.description: Read progress file for a given job_id.

known_limitations: Third-party EditorApplication.Exit hooks can kill IPC-lane builds; use --batch to avoid.
Target / scripting-define switches need manual editor restart or --batch with -buildTarget.
build_cancel is cooperative at queue stage only; BuildPipeline.BuildPlayer has no interrupt API.
Non-terminal progress files are not auto-recovered; clear Library/unictl-builds/ and re-run.
Avoid running two unictl-initiated batch Unity instances on the same project.
